what: Committed notebook fix + loop records 001-003 as commit 96b500b1 on polly-nav-clean, pushed to origin, reloaded Colab GitHub-preview via Playwright, confirmed both Cell 4 and Cell 12 render the fixed content in the public preview path.

git.commit_subject: fix(notebooks): stabilize governance gate colab live demo
git.stage_strategy: specific paths (NOT git add -A) to avoid sweeping ~20 other WIP files in the working tree
git.notebook_diff: exactly 2 insertions + 2 deletions (the two edited cells)

playwright.page_title: SCBE Governance Gate - Live Demo API - Colab

cell_4_rendered_lines: !cd {REPO_DIR} && git fetch origin {BRANCH} && git checkout {BRANCH} && git pull origin {BRANCH}
# CLAUDE.md warns that sys.path.insert(0, 'src/') causes a dual-package collision
cell_12_rendered_lines: # Preferred: add NGROK_AUTH_TOKEN as a Colab secret (left sidebar, key icon)
NGROK_AUTH_TOKEN = ""  # <-- fallback only; prefer Colab secrets

playwright.console_error_classification: browser-side 'not signed into Google' signals, IDENTICAL to loop record 002, NOT notebook execution errors

why.commit_scope_reason: Working tree has ~20 other files in flight (docs, tests, training scripts, skills). git add -A would entangle the notebook fix with unrelated work and make the commit hard to revert or cherry-pick. Specific-path staging keeps this commit surgical.
why.push_purpose: The Colab URL https://colab.research.google.com/github/.../blob/polly-nav-clean/... serves whatever is currently on the polly-nav-clean branch. Until the push lands, the user's browser sees the OLD broken notebook. Pushing closes the static-vs-runtime drift window for any future Colab visitor.
why.playwright_verify_reason: File-on-disk correctness does NOT prove the fix is visible through the public preview. GitHub caches, CDN latency, and branch protection could all silently eat the change. A Playwright snapshot against the live Colab URL is the only way to prove end-to-end: local fix -> commit -> push -> GitHub -> Colab GitHub-preview -> visible.
why.design_principle: Every successful flight is recorded as a structured JSON record (not a string), with what/how/why separated. When a future flight returns to this task and diverges, the merge rule compresses identical fields into common and keeps differences as wings. This record is generation 0 (first flight) so common and wings are empty.
